name: Universal Regex & Check-Character Validator — IDs, codes & patterns
description: Live, as-you-type validation for the checks REDCap cannot enforce at entry. FIVE rule kinds, which COMPOSE on the same field: @UVALIDATE — IDs/codes minted with check characters (ISO 7064, Damm, Verhoeff, Luhn, GS1/ABA/MRZ/ISBN; single fields or pooled several-IDs-in-one-box), or any regex-shaped code with progressive what's-still-missing guidance (Text/Notes). @UVASSERT — cross-field constraints: the field is invalid unless a condition holds, e.g. [end_date]>=[start_date], [dose]<=[max_dose], or a confirm-field ([id]=[id_confirm]) — branching only hides, a range check only warns, and Data Quality is batch, so none of them can block a bad relationship at entry. @UVREQUIRED — required with the two things REDCap's own required flag lacks: a condition and a real block. @UVUNIQUE — no duplicates across records (project/DAG/event scope, optional composite keys), checked live against the server; REDCap has no native field-level uniqueness. @UVCHOICES — dynamic choice filtering: show/hide individual options of a radio, dropdown or checkbox field while a condition holds (cascading country→region→site lists without duplicate fields; REDCap's @HIDECHOICE is static). A selection that becomes hidden is never cleared — it is flagged and can block the save. @UVASSERT/@UVREQUIRED/@UVUNIQUE also work on dropdown, radio, yes-no, true-false and slider fields. Enforcement happens in the BROWSER, per rule: message, confirm, or block the form/survey save; a post-save hook logs violations to the module log as a best-effort audit wherever REDCap fires redcap_save_record — for values that arrive by API or Data Import (where hook coverage is version-dependent), use the module's Validation scan project page, which re-runs every rule over every saved record on demand and exports the violations as CSV. Three ways to configure, mixable per rule: the settings dialog (one rule, many fields), fast field-name entry, or field annotations for bulk setup from the data dictionary.

authors.name: CHPR
authors.institution: Bamenda Center for Health Promotion and Research (CHPR)

action-tags.description: Validate this Text or Notes field with the Universal Regex & Check-Character Validator, from the Online Designer or the data dictionary CSV instead of the module's Configure dialog. Forms: @UVALIDATE (default ISO 7064 Mod 37,36 check), @UVALIDATE=verhoeff (pick an algorithm), or a full-rule JSON such as @UVALIDATE={"algorithm":"none","pattern":"FC[0-9]{4}","blockSave":"hard"} or @UVALIDATE={"type":"pooled","expectedIds":3}. Algorithm names accept shorthands so you need not spell out the full name: e.g. @UVALIDATE=3736 (or 37,36, mod37_36) = ISO 7064 Mod 37,36, 9710 = Mod 97,10, 112 = Mod 11,2, letters1/letters2, mod10 = Luhn, gs1 = GS1 Mod-10, aba = ABA Mod-10, mrz = ICAO MRZ Mod-10, isbn = Weighted Mod-11, and regex (or format) = no check character. JSON keys: type, algorithm, source, pattern, strip, keepChars, idLengths, idMinLen, idMaxLen, expectedIds, blockSave, when, note. The optional when key makes the rule conditional — it validates only while a REDCap-style condition is true, e.g. @UVALIDATE={"algorithm":"verhoeff","when":"[specimen_type]='2'"} (supports [field] and [checkbox(code)] refs, = <> != > < >= <=, and/or/not, parentheses; a false condition skips the rule, it does NOT erase the value). One field may carry SEVERAL tags when each has a different when (plus at most one without, as the fallback) — branched validation. suggestFix (true/false, default false) opts in to the "should end in X" check-character hint. Fields with identical tags are grouped into one rule; a malformed tag shows a configuration error.

Conditional REQUIRED: the field must not be left blank — with a real save block and a condition, neither of which REDCap's own required flag offers. Forms: @UVREQUIRED (always required), @UVREQUIRED="[consent]='1'" (required only WHILE the condition is true — the value is the condition), or JSON @UVREQUIRED={"when":"[consent]='1'","message":"Phone needed for consented participants","blockSave":"hard"}. The condition uses the same REDCap-style dialect as when. A blank (or whitespace-only) field shows the notice while the requirement is in force; filling it clears the notice — required mode never judges the VALUE, so pair it with @UVALIDATE or @UVASSERT (modes compose, all must pass). Works on Text, Notes, dropdown, radio, yes/no, true/false and slider fields (not calc — the person entering data cannot fill a calc). Several @UVREQUIRED tags with different conditions branch (plus at most one without, as the fallback). Read-only fields show the notice but never block the save. A malformed tag shows a configuration error under the field. The server audit logs a blank-while-required save as type 'required'.

No-duplicates across records — a validation REDCap has no native field-level equivalent for. As the value is typed, the module asks the server (a CSRF-protected module AJAX call) whether it is already recorded in another record, and shows used/free live; enforcement modes are the usual message/confirm/block. Forms: @UVUNIQUE (unique across the project), @UVUNIQUE=event (scope shorthand: project, dag or event), or JSON @UVUNIQUE={"with":["site"],"scope":"event","message":"This specimen ID is already registered","blockSave":"hard","when":"...","surveys":true}. with makes the key COMPOSITE (the value plus those fields together must be unique, e.g. specimen ID within site). Scopes: project (default), dag (unique within each Data Access Group), event (unique within the same event). surveys is an explicit OPT-IN and should stay off unless you have thought about it: survey respondents are not logged in, so an "already used" answer tells anyone holding the survey link that a specific value is in this study, one value at a time. The module refuses the opt-in on fields REDCap flags as Identifiers (there, it would let a stranger test whether a named person is enrolled); elsewhere surveys get a yes/no only, never a record id, and the unauthenticated path is rate-limited. Staff see the colliding record id only when it is in their own DAG. Works on Text, Notes, dropdown, radio, yes/no, true/false and slider fields. Two near-simultaneous saves can both pass the live check — the post-save audit logs such a collision (type 'unique'), so review the module log for races. A malformed tag shows a configuration error under the field.

Dynamic CHOICE FILTERING: show or hide individual options of a radio, dropdown or checkbox field while a REDCap-style condition holds — cascading lists (country → region → site) without creating near-duplicate fields; REDCap's own @HIDECHOICE is static. JSON form only: @UVCHOICES={"when":"[country]='1'","show":["101","102"]} (whitelist — every other code hides) or @UVCHOICES={"when":"[legacy]<>'1'","hide":["9"]} (blacklist), plus optional message and blockSave (off/confirm/hard). Repeat the tag with different when conditions to branch (one tag per country, plus at most one without a condition as the fallback); no active branch means no filter, all choices shown. The codes must exist in the field's own choice list. A currently-selected choice that becomes hidden is never cleared — it stays visible, is flagged invalid with the message, and blockSave decides whether the save is challenged; dropdowns keep the stale option visible but disabled. A value outside the choice list (e.g. a missing-data code) is out of scope and never flagged. Works on radio, dropdown and checkbox fields (not yes/no, true/false, sql, or matrix fields). The post-save audit logs a saved hidden choice as type 'choices' (reason 'hidden-choice') and the Validation scan reports it. A malformed tag shows a configuration error under the field.

Cross-field CONSTRAINT: the field is invalid unless a condition is true — the gap REDCap branching (only hides), min/max (only warns) and Data Quality (batch, no as-you-type block) leave open. Forms: @UVASSERT="[end_date]>=[start_date]" (the condition is the value), or JSON @UVASSERT={"assert":"[end_date]>=[start_date]","message":"End must be on or after start","blockSave":"hard"}. Confirm-a-value ("type it twice") is just @UVASSERT="[id]=[id_confirm]". The condition uses the same REDCap-style dialect as when ([field] and [checkbox(code)] refs, = <> != > < >= <=, and/or/not, parentheses; ISO dates and numbers compare correctly). JSON keys: assert (required), message (your wording, shown on failure), blockSave (off/confirm/hard), when (only enforce the constraint while this is true). An EMPTY field is inert — use @UVREQUIRED to require a value. Works on Text, Notes, dropdown, radio, yes/no, true/false, calc and slider fields. A field may carry @UVASSERT alongside @UVALIDATE (they COMPOSE — both must pass) and several @UVASSERT tags with different when conditions branch (plus at most one without, as the fallback). A malformed tag shows a configuration error under the field.

project-settings.name:
Universal Regex & Check-Character Validator
Add ONE rule per kind of validation — a rule can cover any number of fields. Four kinds of rule: Single value / Pooled (check-character or regex ID validation), Constraint (cross-field: invalid unless a condition is true) and Required (must not be blank, optionally only while a condition is true). Three ways to attach fields to a rule: the field picker (click its + to add more fields), the fast-entry box (type field names separated by commas), or skip this dialog entirely and tag fields with @UVALIDATE, @UVASSERT, @UVREQUIRED, @UVUNIQUE or @UVCHOICES in the Online Designer's Action Tags box / the data dictionary CSV (bulk setup — see the documentation). Dynamic choice filtering (@UVCHOICES — cascading radio/dropdown/checkbox options) is annotation-only in this version. Different rule kinds may cover the SAME field (they compose — all must pass). Leave a rule's advanced boxes blank to use the defaults.

How to log invalid values caught by the post-save audit. Every mode except 'off' logs field, instrument, event and instance; the modes differ in how the invalid VALUE and the RECORD ID are stored. 'Hashed' is a keyed, project-scoped HMAC (module-held secret) — that is pseudonymization, not anonymity: treat the module log as identifying data for access and retention purposes.

choices.name: Keyed hash of the value + raw record ID (default — correlates repeats within this project without storing the value; the record ID stays readable so staff can fix the record)
Strict: no value, record ID as keyed hash (minimal-identifier mode for sites where record IDs are themselves identifying)
Raw value + raw record ID (only if your data-governance policy allows identifiers in module logs)
Do not log detections at all (audit ERRORS are still logged, without any record identifier)

project-settings.name: Debug: include exception messages in audit-error log entries. Leave OFF in production — exception text can quote data values.

sub_settings.name: Rule label (optional — your own name for this rule, e.g. 'Specimen IDs' or 'Legacy screening codes')
What this rule checks

choices.name: Single value (one ID/code per field — check character and/or format)
Pooled (several IDs jammed into one field)
Constraint (cross-field rule: the field is invalid unless a condition is true — set the condition below; algorithm/pattern boxes are ignored)
Required (the field must not be left blank — optionally only while the 'Only validate when' condition is true; algorithm/pattern boxes are ignored)
Unique (no duplicates across records, checked live against the server — see the Unique boxes below; algorithm/pattern boxes are ignored)

sub_settings.name: Field(s) this rule validates — click + to add more fields to this same rule
Fast entry (optional): more field names for this rule, separated by commas or spaces. Merged with the pickers above; unknown names show a configuration error.

Only validate when (optional): a REDCap-style condition such as [specimen_type]='2' or [consent(1)]='1' and [site]<>'9'. Supports [field] and [checkbox(code)] references, = <> != > < >= <=, and/or/not, and parentheses; functions, [event][field] prefixes and smart variables are not supported. Fields on this instrument are read live as they are edited; a field on another instrument is resolved on the server against its saved value and only the result is sent to the browser (its value never appears in the page). While the condition is false the rule is skipped entirely — the value is NOT erased (unlike field branching). Several rules may cover the SAME field when each has a different condition (plus at most one without, as the fallback) — the rule whose condition is true validates the field.

Constraint rules only: the condition that must be TRUE for the field's value to be valid, e.g. [end_date]>=[start_date] or [dose]<=[max_dose]. Same REDCap-style dialect as 'Only validate when' ([field] and [checkbox(code)] references, = <> != > < >= <=, and/or/not, parentheses; ISO dates and numbers compare correctly). An EMPTY field is never tested — use a Required rule to demand a value (one field may carry both; the rules compose).

Constraint and Required rules only (optional): your wording shown when the rule fails, e.g. 'End date must be on or after the start date'. Strongly recommended for constraints — only you can word what the relationship means. Left blank, a generic message is shown.

Unique rules only (optional): composite-key field names, separated by commas — the value plus these fields TOGETHER must be unique (e.g. a specimen ID within its site). Up to 5 fields; each must exist and hold one scalar value.
Unique rules only: where the value must be unique.

Unique rules only: also check live on SURVEY pages. OFF by default, and think before ticking it: survey respondents are not logged in, so an "already used" answer tells anyone holding your survey link that a specific value is already in this study — they can test values one at a time. Never enable this on anything that identifies a person (a national ID, phone or MRN would let a stranger check whether someone is enrolled); the module refuses it outright on fields REDCap flags as Identifiers. Reasonable use: a non-identifying response token, to stop the same person submitting twice. Surveys always get a yes/no only, never a record id, and are rate-limited. Leaving this off costs nothing in data quality — survey submissions are still covered by the post-save audit and the Validation scan.
Check-character method — must match how the IDs were minted. Validating a plain format/code with no check character? Pick 'No check character' and set the format pattern below.

choices.name: ISO 7064 Mod 11,2 — digits, check 0-9 or X
ABA Mod-10 — digits, 1 char (US routing style)
ICAO MRZ Mod-10 — digits ONLY, 1 char (7,3,1 passport scheme; not for letter-bearing MRZ fields)
Weighted Mod-11 — digits, 1 char incl. X (ISBN-10 style; full strength only up to 9 digits — prefer Mod 11,2 for longer IDs)

sub_settings.name: What the check runs over

Suggest the correct final check character on a mismatch (OFF by default — a visible "should end in X" hint can entice staff to force-fit a mistyped ID instead of re-scanning it; enable only where that risk is acceptable)

Optional format pattern (regex for ONE ID; printable ASCII; tested against the UPPERCASED, dash-unified value). Required if method is 'No check character'. JavaScript regex syntax; catastrophic patterns — nested quantifiers, repeated ambiguous groups, and overlapping unbounded quantifiers such as (a+)+, (a|aa)+, or .*.* — are rejected at save time.
Separators to ignore before checking (default: - / space _ | \ )
Pooled only: extra characters to keep when splitting a pooled field (rarely needed; printable ASCII, up to 64)
Pooled only: exact ID length(s), comma-separated (e.g. 10 or 10, 12; up to 32 values, each up to 64). Overrides the min/max range below.
Pooled only: minimum ID length (default 8). Used only if exact lengths are blank.
Pooled only: maximum ID length (default 14; must be less than 2× the minimum; up to 64).
Pooled only: expected number of IDs in the field (optional; warns if different). Positive whole number up to 9999.
On an invalid value (all three are BROWSER behaviors; API and import writes cannot be blocked — they are only logged per the audit-log setting above)

choices.name: Advisory — warn and ask before the browser saves
Compulsory — block the browser form/survey save until fixed (never blocks API/import writes; skipped on read-only fields)
